topic: SAA Exponential Cell Separation — c5 change-driven verification of c4 hole #1 patch (LLSVP-preemption paragraph added to detail_verdict_text)
change_summary: detail_verdict_text grew 1090 → 1702 chars (+56%) since c4 review (2026-04-22). Diff vs git revision at c4 timestamp shows decider added a new 3-sentence paragraph between the original opener and the post-hoc trap: 'The mechanism gap is asymmetric: globe-model MHD provides reversed-flux patches at the core-mantle boundary and westward-drift advection from core flow — deriving directional asymmetry from known physical processes. The dome labels the same phenomenon "aetheric vortex decay" with no derivation of azimuthal asymmetry and no published functional form (50.6° is hardcoded; the exponential rate parameter τ is unpublished). Acknowledging the bifurcation rate as observational does not reduce our credibility — both sides use the same number, and the standard model explains the mechanism while the dome does not.' This is a partial response to c4 hole #1's preemptive_recommendation. detail_claim was also fixed (now prefixed with 'The dome model asserts that' — addresses c4 hole #2). detail_evidence (1351 chars), claim, finding, verdict, code_analysis tags, TLDRs all unchanged.
our_argument_summary: Std Model Explains. Standard MHD via Terra-Nova et al. (2017) provides the spatial mechanism for SAA bifurcation through reversed-flux patches at the core–mantle boundary; westward drift ~0.2–0.3°/year is flow-driven advection in a rotating spherical shell; African-vs-American decay asymmetry is attributable to the African Large Low-Shear-Velocity Province (LLSVP) modulating local core flow. Dome has no MHD equations, no fluid mechanism, no published τ for its 'exponential' label, and a radially symmetric aetheric vortex incompatible with observed azimuthal structure. Bifurcation rate (30.8°→50.6° over 25 years, ~0.79°/yr) is observational on both sides; verdict is backed by the post-hoc trap (CHAOS-7 publicly available, no independent timestamp pre-registration). c5 verifies that the c4 LLSVP-preemption recommendation was substantially honored (gist captured) but the highest-leverage phrasing — explicit LLSVP attribution and 'fatal-to-radial-symmetry / regardless-of-prediction-timing' framing — was attenuated.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Re-verified from c4: 50.57° hardcoded as architectural constant; live-fetch pipeline tracks SAA decay RATE (≥28 nT/yr at INTERMAGNET stations, → WIN-037/042) — angular separation has no live-fetch path. PRED-013's 30-year-horizon 120–180° band is a manual long-horizon claim, not auto-monitored.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: 50.57° remains hardcoded in 05-model.txt. The two endpoints (30.8° in 2000, 50.6° in 2025) define the dome's claimed curve; with only two points, exponential / linear / quadratic / log fits are mathematically indistinguishable. No τ has been published in the c4→c5 interval.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Unchanged from c4: unpublished τ remains the central rhetorical escape; without a functional form the dome can post-hoc fit any 2055 outcome inside the 120–180° band as 'confirmation' and any outside as 'aetheric damping' or 'overshoot.'
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None new. The c3-era live_fetch / hardcoded inconsistency remains resolved (c4).

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Re-verified in clone at run start. raw-text/05-model.txt line 43 carries 50.57° as a static architectural constant; raw-text/08-live-power.txt tracks SAA decay (rate metric, WIN-037/042 family), not angular separation. WIN-005 (paired SAA citation) shares hardcoded tag — cross-WIN consistent.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Terra-Nova et al. (2017) PEPI, DOI 10.1016/j.pepi.2017.03.002, derives SAA bifurcation from reversed-flux patches and core flow — published before the dome model's prediction commits. The dome relabels the cause as 'aetheric vortex decay' without altering any predicted value.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: CHAOS-7 (Finlay et al. 2020) is a public dataset documenting SAA bifurcation prior to the dome's monitor commit. Author's own predictions-page definition requires timestamping before confirming-data pull; the monitor script hardcodes the target on the same commit as the data-fetch line, which makes the temporal ordering unverifiable from repo history. Public-data carve-out of Part 3.1b does not apply.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Re-verified: PROS-001 publishes 'exponential separation curve' as a label without a τ. The c5 patch added 'the exponential rate parameter τ is unpublished' to detail_verdict_text — formalizing the c4 stronger-argument observation. No derivation path from disc radius / firmament height / aether coupling κ to the 50.6° value exists.

holes_found.description: c4 hole #1 was PARTIALLY addressed but the punchiest framing was attenuated. The decider added a 3-sentence paragraph to detail_verdict_text capturing the GIST of the c4 preemptive_recommendation (asymmetric mechanism gap, dome has no derivation of azimuthal asymmetry, exponential τ unpublished, bifurcation rate observational on both sides). What was DROPPED in patch translation: (a) explicit LLSVP attribution — the verdict now says 'westward-drift advection from core flow' but does not name the Large Low-Shear-Velocity Province by name, even though the detail_evidence already explains it; (b) explicit 'fatal to a radially symmetric aetheric vortex' framing — the verdict says 'no derivation of azimuthal asymmetry' which is softer and risks being read as a definitional gap rather than a structural impossibility; (c) explicit 'regardless of prediction timing' close — the new text doesn't frame the asymmetry argument as timing-independent, leaving the post-hoc trap and the asymmetry argument rhetorically separated. The defense identified in c4's advocate_mode (defense_survives=4) is now slightly weaker but not closed: a defender can still pivot on 'both sides retrofit the rate' because the new paragraph treats the asymmetry argument as parallel to the rate-acknowledgment instead of as the structural-falsification it actually is.
holes_found.recommendation: Append one parenthetical clause inside the new paragraph, immediately after 'no derivation of azimuthal asymmetry': '(MHD attributes the African–American asymmetry to LLSVP-modulated core flow — a structural mechanism the dome\'s radially symmetric aetheric vortex cannot reproduce, regardless of when the prediction was made).' This pulls the LLSVP detail forward from detail_evidence into the verdict synthesis and closes the timing-independence loop in 30 words. Do not rewrite the paragraph; surgical insertion only.

holes_found.description: c4 hole #3 still un-addressed. detail_evidence retains the unsourced supporting claim 'is consistent with westward-drift advection in broader MHD models' with no specific paper cited. Terra-Nova et al. (2017) is the only DOI in detail_evidence and it is cited only for the spatial mechanism, not for a published bifurcation-rate prediction. A defender can still ask 'name the MHD paper that publishes the 0.8°/year separation rate prediction' and our text has no citation handle. This hole survived the c4→c5 patch round because the decider chose to address verdict-text framing (hole #1) rather than evidence-text sourcing (hole #3).
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) substitute Aubert (2015) 'Geomagnetic forecasts driven by thermal wind dynamics in Earth\'s core' (Geophys. J. Int. 203:1738-1751, DOI 10.1093/gji/ggv394) — published rate-style geomagnetic forecasts using core-flow MHD — or (b) soften phrasing to 'is consistent with the westward-drift advection regime modeled by core-flow MHD' to drop the implicit promise of a specific rate-prediction paper. Option (b) is the lower-risk patch.

holes_found.description: c4 stronger-argument #1 (PROMOTE LLSVP AZIMUTHAL ASYMMETRY INTO SUMMARY-ROW finding) remains un-integrated through two patch rounds (c3→c4→c5). Current finding (195 chars): 'MHD core dynamics explain SAA bifurcation mechanism (reversed-flux patches at CMB); dome has no fluid mechanism; bifurcation rate in detail evidence is observational, not first-principles-derived.' Readers who never expand the panel see the symmetric concession but not the asymmetric kill-shot. This is the same hole c4 flagged; survival across two cycles suggests the recommendation needs to be re-pitched to the decider as a finding-string surgical edit rather than a freeform expansion. Severity is minor not moderate because the punchier paragraph IS now in detail_verdict_text — readers who do expand will see the argument; only TLDR/skim readers miss it.
holes_found.recommendation: Append one clause to finding (no rewrite): '... not first-principles-derived; African–American decay asymmetry falsifies a radially symmetric aetheric vortex regardless of prediction timing.' Brings finding from 195 → ~285 chars; preserves all existing content. This single edit converts the summary row from 'mechanism-comparison' framing to 'structural-falsification' framing — the kill-shot becomes visible without expanding.

holes_found.description: c4 stronger-argument #3 (PRED-013 FEASIBILITY MATH in detail_extra) remains un-integrated; detail_extra is still null. This is the explicit 30-year arithmetic: linear extrapolation = 74.4° in 2055, PRED-013 lower bound 120° requires 2.92× current rate, upper bound 180° requires 5.45× current rate. The math shows the dome's most testable long-horizon commitment is currently consistent with but not predicting the observed trajectory — a credit-and-debit framing the verdict text could use but doesn't currently surface. Survival across two cycles; severity minor (not blocking; the math is in the c4/c5 review record and any analyst regenerating prose will pick it up).

stronger_arguments.description: Recommend two surgical edits this run rather than a fresh major rewrite, because the c4→c5 patch demonstrated the decider can land surgical paragraph insertions: (1) the LLSVP/timing parenthetical inside the new detail_verdict_text paragraph (hole #1); (2) the 'African–American decay asymmetry falsifies a radially symmetric aetheric vortex regardless of prediction timing' clause appended to finding (hole #3-list-item-3). Together these elevate the asymmetry argument from 'present in evidence' to 'present in summary AND in verdict synthesis' — full-coverage across reader paths.

Aubert (2015) GJI 203:1738-1751 (DOI 10.1093/gji/ggv394) is the strongest available citation for an MHD-published rate-style forecast using core-flow dynamics; if it lands cleanly it converts hole #2 (unsourced 'broader MHD models') from a soften-the-language patch into a strengthen-the-text patch. Worth the analyst's verification before accepting the soften-only fallback.

kernel_of_truth.description: The c4→c5 patch is a measurable improvement to the WIN's rhetorical architecture. The new detail_verdict_text paragraph (data/wins.json:WIN-004.detail_verdict_text) lifts the mechanism-asymmetry argument out of detail_evidence and into the verdict synthesis where readers who expand the panel will encounter it. The c4 hole #2 (detail_claim attribution prefix) was cleanly addressed — current detail_claim now reads 'The dome model asserts that globe models have no mechanism for SAA bifurcation' (data/wins.json:WIN-004.detail_claim), which is the c4 recommendation honored verbatim. The decider also picked up c4's 'τ is unpublished' framing (data/wins.json:WIN-004.detail_verdict_text) — that single phrase converts the dome's 'exponential' label from a rhetorical posture into an explicit testability gap. The patch demonstrates the decider can land paragraph-level edits surgically without disturbing surrounding fields.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Even with the c5 improvements, the verdict 'Std Model Explains' still rests on the structural argument that MHD has a mechanism the dome lacks, not on first-principles rate derivation by either side — and the c5 review re-flags that the punchiest version of that argument (LLSVP attribution + radial-symmetry falsification + timing-independence) survives in detail_evidence (data/wins.json:WIN-004.detail_evidence) but is rendered softer in detail_verdict_text. A defender pivoting on 'both sides retrofit the rate' is partially defused by the new paragraph but not fully closed; full closure requires the LLSVP/timing parenthetical recommended in hole #1. Holes #1, #2, #3 (minor) and the un-integrated stronger-arguments do not warrant a verdict change — they warrant the surgical edits enumerated above. Verdict 'Std Model Explains' remains warranted (data/wins.json:WIN-004.verdict).

advocate_mode.best_defense: After the c5 patch, a fair-minded dome defender pivots more narrowly than in c4 but the pivot still has teeth: 'Your new paragraph claims MHD provides azimuthal asymmetry from core flow — but the support is buried in your own detail_evidence (LLSVP), not in the verdict synthesis. Your verdict says the standard model has "derivation of asymmetry" but Terra-Nova et al. (2017) — which is your only cited authority — derives the SPATIAL MECHANISM (flux patches at CMB), not the AZIMUTHAL ASYMMETRY (which requires the LLSVP layer you don\'t cite explicitly in the verdict). So your verdict claims more than your verdict-side citations support. We at least named our claim "exponential" qualitatively before 2025 — your acknowledgment that "both sides use the same number" cuts both ways: neither side has staked a quantitative rate prediction in advance. The remaining gap is a verdict-text-vs-detail-evidence integration gap on YOUR side, not a model-physics gap on ours.' This defense is weaker than c4's because the patch closed the rate-acknowledgment-asymmetry door; it remains live on the LLSVP-citation-coverage axis.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Adopt hole #1's surgical edit: insert LLSVP+timing-independence parenthetical inside the new detail_verdict_text paragraph. After that single insertion, the defense rating drops to 1–2 (defender has to either accept the structural-falsification framing or argue Terra-Nova should be replaced with Aubert (2015) for rate-style forecasts — at which point we adopt hole #2 option (a) and the defense closes entirely).

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: None new. WIN-004 / WIN-005 still share monitoring='hardcoded', relabels_standard=true, post_hoc=true, derives_from_dome=false (cluster consistent). WIN-040 (SAA western boundary <45°W) was patched on 2026-04-23/24 (commits c4bfd80, a3bf2c4) to refine longitude-cell-centre numbers and to make the H(r) zero-azimuthal-derivative point explicit — this strengthens WIN-004's 'radially symmetric aetheric vortex cannot produce azimuthal structure' argument because WIN-040 now articulates the same H(r) constraint independently. Recommend the analyst cross-link WIN-004 ↔ WIN-040 in a future expansion (out of scope for c5; flagged for tracker).

claims_checked: Dome hardcoded 50.57° vs observed 50.6° — agree to within 0.03° (post-hoc fit signature)
detail_verdict_text length: claimed 1702 in fingerprint vs measured 1702 — exact match
DOI 10.1016/j.pepi.2017.03.002 — Terra-Nova et al. (2017) PEPI 266:39-53, re-verified resolves to 'Relating the South Atlantic Anomaly and geomagnetic flux patches'
quantitative_verification.computations: Ran arithmetic in node REPL inside the clone. (50.6-30.8)/25 = 0.7920 °/yr → matches text. Linear 30-year extrapolation 50.6 + 0.7920*30 = 74.36° → matches c4. (120-50.6)/30 = 2.3133 °/yr → 2.92× current — matches c4 to 2 sig figs. (180-50.6)/30 = 4.3133 °/yr → 5.45× current — matches c4 to 2 sig figs. detail_verdict_text byte length re-measured at 1702. No new numeric claims were added in the c4→c5 interval beyond 'τ is unpublished' (qualitative).

deeper_analysis: This is a change-driven verification pass triggered by detail_verdict_text growth (1090 → 1702 chars, +56%) since c4 review on 2026-04-22. Diff-against-git shows the growth is fully explained by one paragraph insertion addressing c4 hole #1's preemptive_recommendation. The patch is a real improvement: the rhetorical asymmetry between 'mechanism-vs-rate' is now explicit in the verdict synthesis rather than only in evidence, and 'τ is unpublished' is now a phrase the verdict carries. But the patch translation lost the punchiest c4-recommended framing (LLSVP attribution by name, 'fatal to radial symmetry,' 'regardless of prediction timing') — the gist survived but the kill-shot weakened. c4 hole #2 was cleanly addressed (detail_claim now has 'The dome model asserts that' prefix). c4 hole #3 (unsourced 'broader MHD models' in detail_evidence) and stronger-arguments #1 (LLSVP into finding) and #3 (PRED-013 math into detail_extra) were NOT addressed — survival across two patch cycles. Recommended action minor_edit, not major_rewrite, because all surviving issues are textual rather than analytical and each maps to a sentence-or-phrase-level surgical edit. Verdict 'Std Model Explains' continues to hold; advocate_mode defense_survives drops from c4=4 to c5=3 (improvement) but does not yet reach 1–2 (full closure).

citations_verified: DOI 10.1016/j.pepi.2017.03.002 — Terra-Nova et al. (2017), 'Relating the South Atlantic Anomaly and geomagnetic flux patches,' Phys. Earth Planet. Inter. — DOI re-checked, still resolves correctly.
raw-text/05-model.txt line 43 'SAA separation (2025): 50.57 deg' — re-verified in clone; hardcoded as architectural constant.
raw-text/02-wins.txt PROS-001 — re-verified: dome publishes the qualitative label 'exponential separation curve' with no τ.
raw-text/08-live-power.txt — re-verified: 20-domain live-fetch list tracks SAA decay rate (77 nT/yr), not angular separation.
citations_unchecked: Aubert (2015) Geophys. J. Int. 203:1738-1751 — proposed in stronger-arguments #2; not yet retrieved/verified. Decider/analyst should retrieve before substituting into detail_evidence.
https://john09289.github.io/predictions live-site link in detail_verdict_text — not re-checked against archive; non-blocking.
